prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `8719f442`.
codex_response:
Implemented `8719f442` end to end. The rule is: treat the 3x3 input as a motif; each foreground cell creates a solid 3x3 block in the corresponding shifted position of a 5x5 block lattice, and it also creates one or two outward copies of the original motif depending on whether that cell is offset vertically, horizontally, or diagonally from the center.

I used the Opus summary only as an initial hint and discarded the Sonnet summary because it implied fixed replica placements; the actual rule is local per foreground cell. That correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8719f442/notes.md#L1). No new primitive was needed. The generator keeps the official 3x3 motif structure, uses sparse 3-5 cell patterns, and varies the foreground color on a zero background, which gives enough diversity while staying close to the task family.
